title: issues block_kind prefix is 'issue-' (lowercase) in samples/conception.json — violates DEC-0027 (uppercase canonical IDs); conception itself disagrees with its own enacted decision
description: Verified 2026-05-28 via direct read of packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json: the `issues` block_kind declares `prefix: "issue-"` (lowercase). DEC-0027 (enacted as part of the theatre-trio resolution earlier this session) mandates uppercase canonical IDs across the substrate vocabulary, with migrate-forward semantics for legacy lowercase. Every other prefixed block_kind in conception follows uppercase (TASK-, DEC-, FEAT-, FGAP-, REQ-, STORY-, R-, REVIEW-, VER-, RAT-, PHASE-, CTX-, WO-, PLAN-). `issues` is the lone non-conformer. The conception ships and is adopted via /context accept-all as a downstream project's config baseline — any downstream substrate following the conception inherits the DEC-0027 violation at install time.

evidence.reference: issues entry: prefix='issue-' (lowercase) — contrast with all 14 other prefixed kinds which are uppercase
DEC-0027 enacted — canonical IDs uppercase; legacy lowercase migrate forward

impact: conception ships its own DEC-violation. Downstream consumers running /context accept-all adopt non-canonical vocabulary. Migration tooling and validators that check DEC-0027 conformance will flag adopting projects as non-compliant by virtue of using the canonical-shipped conception. In-pi agents enumerating canonical_ids from conception report 'issue-' verbatim (as observed in P-C1 telemetry response 2026-05-28) — propagating the violation to operator-facing surface.
canonical_vocabulary: DEC-0027 uppercase canonical IDs; conception-as-canon-baseline; canonical-self-consistency
proposed_resolution: Migrate the issues prefix in samples/conception.json from 'issue-' to 'ISSUE-' per DEC-0027. Update the issues schema's id-pattern (^ISSUE-\d+$ or equivalent); update any shipped samples/blocks/issues.json items; record a migration entry per the migrate-forward semantics DEC-0027 already prescribes (legacy 'issue-NNNN' resolves to 'ISSUE-NNNN' for read; new items written uppercase). Annotate the conception change as the canon-source-of-truth migration that downstream /context accept-all picks up automatically on the next conception consumption.
